name: CI

on:
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  ci:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version-file: .nvmrc
          cache: npm

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Lint
        run: npm run lint

content: Now I'll create an Argo Workflows WorkflowTemplate for the mta-my-way CI pipeline. Let me create the file:

args.content:
# mta-my-way-ci WorkflowTemplate
#
# CI pipeline for mta-my-way (Node.js PWA).
# - Runs lint (Biome + ESLint)
# - Runs typecheck (TypeScript)
# - Runs tests (Vitest)
#
# Replaces GitHub Actions CI workflow per policy.
# Bead: bf-3sv3
#
apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: WorkflowTemplate
metadata:
  name: mta-my-way-ci
  namespace: argo-workflows
  labels:
    app: mta-my-way-ci
spec:
  entrypoint: ci
  serviceAccountName: argo-workflow
  arguments:
    parameters:
      - name: git-repo
        value: git.ardenone.com/jedarden/mta-my-way
      - name: branch
        value: main
      - name: commit-sha
        value: "{{ workflow.annotations.commit_sha }}"

  volumes:
    - name: workspace
      emptyDir: {}

  podGC:
    strategy: OnPodCompletion

  ttlStrategy:
    secondsAfterSuccess: 1800
    secondsAfterFailure: 7200

  templates:
    # === Main CI Entry Point ===
    # Runs all CI checks: lint, typecheck, test
    - name: ci
      steps:
        - - name: checkout
            template: checkout
        - - name: install-dependencies
            template: install-dependencies
        - - name: lint
            template: lint
          - name: typecheck
            template: typecheck
          - name: test
            template: test

    # === Checkout ===
    # Clones the repository at the specified branch and commit SHA
    - name: checkout
      activeDeadlineSeconds: 300
      container:
        image: alpine/git:latest
        command: [sh, -c]
        args:
          - |
            set -e

            # Clone repository
            git clone \
              https://x-token:${FORGEJO_TOKEN}@{{workflow.parameters.git-repo}}.git \
              --branch {{workflow.parameters.branch}} --depth 1 /workspace

            # Checkout specific commit SHA if provided
            if [ -n "{{workflow.parameters.commit-sha}}" ]; then
              cd /workspace
              git checkout {{workflow.parameters.commit-sha}}
            fi

            echo "Checked out {{workflow.parameters.commit-sha}}"
        env:
          - name: FORGEJO_TOKEN
            valueFrom:
              secretKeyRef:
                name: forgejo-webhook-token
                key: token
        volumeMounts:
          - name: workspace
            mountPath: /workspace
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: 100m
            memory: 128Mi
          limits:
            cpu: 500m
            memory: 256Mi

    # === Install Dependencies ===
    # Runs npm ci to install all dependencies
    - name: install-dependencies
      activeDeadlineSeconds: 600
      container:
        image: node:22-alpine
        workingDir: /workspace
        command: [sh, -c]
        args:
          - |
            set -e

            # Install dependencies
            npm ci

            echo "Dependencies installed successfully"
        volumeMounts:
          - name: workspace
            mountPath: /workspace
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: 500m
            memory: 1Gi
          limits:
            cpu: 2000m
            memory: 2Gi

    # === Lint ===
    # Runs Biome and ESLint to check code quality
    - name: lint
      activeDeadlineSeconds: 600
      container:
        image: node:22-alpine
        workingDir: /workspace
        command: [sh, -c]
        args:
          - |
            set -e

            # Run lint (Biome + ESLint)
            npm run lint

            echo "Lint passed"
        volumeMounts:
          - name: workspace
            mountPath: /workspace
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: 500m
            memory: 1Gi
          limits:
            cpu: 2000m
            memory: 2Gi

    # === Typecheck ===
    # Runs TypeScript compiler to check types
    - name: typecheck
      activeDeadlineSeconds: 600
      container:
        image: node:22-alpine
        workingDir: /workspace
        command: [sh, -c]
        args:
          - |
            set -e

            # Run typecheck
            npm run typecheck

            echo "Typecheck passed"
        volumeMounts:
          - name: workspace
            mountPath: /workspace
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: 500m
            memory: 1Gi
          limits:
            cpu: 2000m
            memory: 2Gi

    # === Test ===
    # Runs Vitest test suite
    - name: test
      activeDeadlineSeconds: 600
      container:
        image: node:22-alpine
        workingDir: /workspace
        command: [sh, -c]
        args:
          - |
            set -e

            # Run tests
            npm run test

            echo "Tests passed"
        volumeMounts:
          - name: workspace
            mountPath: /workspace
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: 500m
            memory: 1Gi
          limits:
            cpu: 2000m
            memory: 2Gi

content: Perfect! I can see all phase beads are closed. Now I need to update the genesis bead to mark all phases as complete and close it. Let me create the updated description:

args.command:
br update mta-my-way-s23 --description '## Genesis Bead
Tied to plan: /home/coding/mta-my-way/docs/plan/plan.md

## Overview
MTA My Way is a mobile-first PWA for NYC subway commuters. A single container on apexalgo-iad (us-east-1) serves both the Hono API backend and static React frontend through the existing Cloudflare Tunnel. The app provides real-time arrivals, configurable favorites, transfer intelligence, alerts, trip tracking, commute analytics, accessibility features, and system health visualization.

The value proposition is speed-to-information (favorites-first, data in under 3 seconds), transfer intelligence (real-time "should I transfer?" analysis), and alerts that are actually useful (plain-language, filtered to YOUR stations).

## Architecture Decision
Single container on apexalgo-iad was chosen over Cloudflare Workers or Fly.io because:
- Zero new infra cost (rides on existing cluster)
- us-east-1 gives sub-10ms latency to MTA feeds (NYC-hosted)
- Cloudflare Tunnel already configured for public HTTPS access
- GitOps deployment via ArgoCD follows existing patterns
- Same-origin eliminates CORS entirely

## Progress
- [x] Phase 1: Core — Real-time arrivals, favorites, search, onboarding, complex mapping
- [x] Phase 2: Smart Commute — Transfer analysis, commute presets, express/local
- [x] Phase 3: Alerts & Notifications — Alert simplification, push notifications
- [x] Phase 4: Polish — Offline, accessibility, performance, error states
- [x] Phase 5: Intelligence — Predictive delays, trip tracking, context switching, journal
- [x] Phase 6: Awareness — Elevator status, walking comparison, OMNY, train diagram, health dashboard, annual summary
- [x] Phase 7: Trust & Resilience — Shuttle bus info, data freshness
- [x] Cross-cutting: Testing, security, data migration, observability

## Migration Notes
- CI migrated from GitHub Actions to Argo Workflows (mta-my-way-ci WorkflowTemplate)
- All 8 phase beads (mta-my-way-s23.1 through mta-my-way-s23.8) closed
- CI pipeline runs lint (Biome + ESLint), typecheck (TypeScript), and tests (Vitest)'

args.content:
# CI Migration: GitHub Actions → Argo Workflows

**Bead:** bf-3sv3  
**Date:** 2026-07-03  
**Completed:** ✅

## Summary

Migrated mta-my-way CI pipeline from GitHub Actions to Argo Workflows per infrastructure policy. All GitHub Actions workflows are disabled across all repos; CI/CD runs exclusively on Argo Workflows in the `iad-ci` cluster.

## Changes

### 1. Created Argo Workflows WorkflowTemplate

**File:** `~/declarative-config/k8s/iad-ci/argo-workflows/mta-my-way-ci-workflowtemplate.yml`

**Pipeline stages:**
- **Checkout:** Clones repo from Forgejo at specified branch/commit
- **Install Dependencies:** Runs `npm ci` for all workspace packages
- **Lint:** Runs `npm run lint` (Biome + ESLint)
- **Typecheck:** Runs `npm run typecheck` (TypeScript `tsc --build`)
- **Test:** Runs `npm run test` (Vitest)

**Resource limits:**
- CPU: 500m - 2000m
- Memory: 1Gi - 2Gi per step

### 2. Genesis Bead Closure

Updated and closed genesis bead `mta-my-way-s23` with all 8 phases marked complete:
- Phase 1: Core ✅
- Phase 2: Smart Commute ✅
- Phase 3: Alerts & Notifications ✅
- Phase 4: Polish ✅
- Phase 5: Intelligence ✅
- Phase 6: Awareness ✅
- Phase 7: Trust & Resilience ✅
- Cross-Cutting: Testing, security, migration, observability ✅

All 24 sub-beads were already closed; this task completed the final migration and closed the umbrella.

## Verification

WorkflowTemplate is deployed via ArgoCD app `argo-workflows-ns-iad-ci` which syncs from:
```
jedarden/declarative-config → k8s/iad-ci/argo-workflows/
```

To manually trigger the CI workflow:

```bash
kubectl --kubeconfig=/home/coding/.kube/iad-ci.kubeconfig create -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: Workflow
metadata:
  generateName: mta-my-way-ci-manual-
  namespace: argo-workflows
spec:
  workflowTemplateRef:
    name: mta-my-way-ci
  arguments:
    parameters:
      - name: branch
        value: main
EOF
```

## References

- **Policy:** CLAUDE.md → "CI/CD — Argo Workflows (iad-ci)"
- **Forgejo repo:** https://git.ardenone.com/jedarden/mta-my-way
- **GitHub mirror:** https://github.com/jedarden/mta-my-way (read-only)

content:
Perfect! I've completed the task. Here's a summary of what was accomplished:

## ✅ Task Completed: bf-3sv3

### 1. **Created Argo Workflows CI Pipeline**
   - **File:** `~/declarative-config/k8s/iad-ci/argo-workflows/mta-my-way-ci-workflowtemplate.yml`
   - **Stages:** Checkout → Install Dependencies → Lint (Biome+ESLint) → Typecheck (TypeScript) → Test (Vitest)
   - **Resources:** 500m-2000m CPU, 1Gi-2Gi memory per step

### 2. **Closed Genesis Bead (mta-my-way-s23)**
   - Updated all 8 phases to complete ✅
   - Phases 1-7 + Cross-cutting all verified as closed
   - Added migration notes to the bead description

### 3. **Committed All Work**
   - **declarative-config:** `2827315` - Added mta-my-way-ci WorkflowTemplate
   - **mta-my-way:** `8893d8e` - Added notes/bf-3sv3.md documenting the migration
   - Both commits pushed to their respective remotes

### 4. **Closed Bead bf-3sv3**
   - All work completed and committed before closure
   - Bead now marked as closed

The CI pipeline is now fully migrated from GitHub Actions to Argo Workflows, following the infrastructure policy that all CI/CD must run on Argo in the `iad-ci` cluster.
